7 hours of sleep before an early morning shift; ends at 6am the next day as required.

diary:
Started the day early with my usual routine—espresso hit just right before the drive to Kirkland. The heat was already building by mid-morning, and I could feel it through the bus windows. Route felt busier than usual; folks were restless in the humidity, I think. Grabbed a sandwich at Whole Foods during my break and tried to stay hydrated.

Afternoon shifts wrapped up okay, nothing dramatic. Headed home to freshen up, and on impulse decided to catch that free concert at Gas Works Park. Didn't expect to go, honestly, but the weather was too nice to waste. Sat there watching the sunset over the water with the Wallingford crowd—all kinds of people spread out on blankets. The music was easy, nothing demanding. Some haze from the wildfires up north, but it didn't ruin anything. Felt good to just be still for a while.
thought_process: I was on autopilot most of the day—thirty years driving routes, you fall into rhythm. But the heat got to me around lunch; I realized I'd been tense holding the wheel in that temperature. The wildfire smoke in the air was subtle but noticeable, made me think about how everything's connected now. After work, instead of going straight home to sit alone, I thought about that concert and decided to actually go. At fifty-six, you learn that spontaneous moments matter more than you think. The decision felt small but right—it shifted my whole evening from just existing to actually living it for a couple hours.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
